content:
@Alice Ryhl did you consider other pointer types for use in List? I find myself needing to thread in an ARef C data structure via either a pointer pair in the private (Rust) part of the structure or reuse of an unused prev/next pair in the C part of the structure.

I am thinking maybe instead of directly using ListArc we can require a generic with trait bound instead? Did you have any thoughts on this already?
I would like to support that. I'm not sure how to best extend the ListArc<_> concept in a generic way, though.
CC @Philipp Stanner who wants to use Box
For Box, the "correct" ListArc type is just Box, since it's already unique.
Maybe we can have a trait where Arc/Box/ARef can specify the ListArc version of themselves?
Using ForeignOwnable implies that it only works with ARef. That's not much better than only working with Arc.

Alice Ryhl said:

Using ForeignOwnable implies that it only works with ARef.

Why is that? Arc is ForeignOwnable as well.
oh, sorry I mixed up with AlwaysRefcounted for a moment
but seems ARef is not ForeignOwnable yet, but it should be reasonable to make it ForeignOwnable.
